FlexDelivery – Integrated Logistics in the Offshore Sector
Kris Vedat, P&O Maritime Logistics Limited, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
Copyright 2023, Society of Petroleum Engineers DOI 10.2118/217024-MS
This paper was prepared for presentation at the ADIPEC held in Abu Dhabi, UAE, 2 – 5 October, 2023.
This paper was selected for presentation by an SPE program committee following review of information contained in an abstract submitted by the author(s). Contents
of the paper have not been reviewed by the Society of Petroleum Engineers and are subject to correction by the author(s). The material does not necessarily reflect

Abstract
This case study aims to showcase how integrated logistics in the offshore sector streamline the
supply chain process, reduce costs, and improves efficiency. The scope of integrated logistics includes
planning, transportation, warehousing, inventory management, and information management, focusing on
collaboration and transparency between all stakeholders in the offshore supply chain.
The process of integrated logistics in the offshore sector begins with the cargo booking. A detailed
logistics plan and schedule are then developed, outlining the supply chain network, transportation modes,
and inventory management strategies. The process is managed by an AI-based platform that automatically
creates short and long-term schedules using various cargo and telemetric data. During the execution
phase, real-time tracking and monitoring of the supply chain process are crucial to managing disruptions.
Continuous improvement is key to optimising the integrated logistics process with a machine learning
element to the logistics tool, resulting in increased efficiency, reduced costs, and improved safety and
reliability.
Implementing integrated logistics in the offshore sector has yielded several positive results. Firstly, it has
improved efficiency in the supply chain process, reducing the time and cost required to move goods and
equipment from the point of origin to the point of consumption. Delivery time has been reduced by 23%,
achieved by using an AI planning system, real-time tracking, and optimised transportation modes.
Secondly, integrated logistics has helped to maintain high levels of safety by reducing the number
of entries into the 500M zone by consolidating cargo and increasing back deck utilisation. Standardised
procedures for logistics operations have been established, minimising the risk of errors and improving
overall safety.
Thirdly, the implementation of integrated logistics has led to increased collaboration and communication
between stakeholders involved in offshore operations, resulting in improved decision-making and reduced
delays, as well as better transparency between all elements of the supply chain.
Real-time tracking and monitoring of the supply chain process have been crucial for effectively managing
disruptions and addressing issues, which is made possible by automating the process using AI, which is
more efficient than manual processes.

SPE-217024-MS
The use of integrated logistics in the offshore sector has resulted in an overall cost reduction of 23% on
the shipment of goods and a reduction of CO2 emissions by 32%, enabling effective management of the
movement of goods and equipment while promoting sustainability.
This approach to integrated offshore logistics will enable effective management of the movement of
goods and equipment from the point of origin to the point of consumption and reduce costs for the oil and
gas sector while ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.
Introduction
As globalisation continues to grow, the importance of efficient logistics management has become
increasingly vital. A company's ability to deliver goods and services in a timely and efficient manner is now
a critical aspect of its competitive advantage. This has necessitated innovation and the implementation of
technology in all sectors, including the offshore sector within the oil and gas industry.
The offshore sector within the oil and gas industry presents unique challenges. Often operating in harsh
and remote environments, the sector must manage complex supply chains, including the transportation of
equipment and materials from various points of origin to the final point of consumption. Any inefficiency
or breakdown in the supply chain can lead to significant delays and cost overruns.
The goal was to re-imagine and reinvent the role of the supply chain, moving away from archaic operating
models towards a more modern, efficient, and environmentally friendly approach. Central to this vision
was the introduction of resource sharing and optimised cargo planning. The concept of shared vessels and
facilities and paying only for space used would bring about economies of scale, reduce waste, and eliminate
duplication of resources.
The solution offers a novel way to solve these challenges by providing an integrated approach to
logistics in the offshore sector. As a comprehensive system, it merges various elements such as planning,
transportation, warehousing, inventory management, and information management. The system aims to
streamline the supply chain process, provide a more flexible logistics method to the oil and gas industry,
reduce costs, and improve efficiency through a strategic and data-driven approach.
At the core of the solution lies artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) technologies. These
technologies are instrumental in managing the vast array of data involved in the supply chain process, from
cargo booking to final delivery and are not new to the industry. AI algorithms are employed to create efficient
schedules, and ML techniques have been used to learn from historical data and continuously improve
processes over a variety of domains [1 – 6].
The system's strength lies in its ability to seamlessly integrate the various elements of logistics
management. The planning phase uses AI to create comprehensive logistics plans. Transportation is
optimised using real-time tracking systems, and inventory management is improved through data-driven
strategies. Warehousing is streamlined with the help of efficient layout plans, and information management
ensures transparency across the entire supply chain process. The focus is not only on individual elements
but also on their interconnectivity to ensure a smooth and efficient process.
Methods
The operational process of the solution begins with booking cargo. This is an essential first step that involves
gathering information about the goods to be transported, including weight, size, quantity, and other specific
handling requirements. This data serves as the foundation for the subsequent steps of the logistics process.
Once the cargo has been booked, an AI-based platform uses the cargo data, along with telemetric
data, to generate an in-depth logistics plan and schedule. This AI-driven system is capable of considering
numerous variables simultaneously and can generate both short and long-term schedules. It optimises
various elements, including route selection, transportation modes, and inventory management strategies.

generated short and long-term schedules, have optimised the transportation process, reducing the delivery
time and, consequently, the cost associated with it.
Secondly, by reducing the number of entries into the 500M zone through cargo consolidation and
increased back deck utilisation, there's a decrease in operating costs. Minimised risk of errors from
standardisation of procedures also reduces the potential for costly mistakes.
Thirdly, better collaboration among stakeholders minimises delays, further reducing costs associated with
prolonged operations or idle times. The ability to coordinate and plan together can lead to shared resources
and consolidated efforts, leading to cost efficiency.
Overall, the system provides an efficient way to reduce the cost per square meter from $650 to $500,
offering substantial savings. In one example, the original cost of $650,000 was optimised to $500,000, a
cost-saving of $150,000, or 23%.
Sustainability
In addition to cost reduction, the solution has made significant strides in enhancing the sustainability of
offshore operations. The digitalised integrated logistics platform has been instrumental in reducing the
environmental impact of operations in several ways.
Firstly, it has resulted in a 32% reduction in CO2 emissions. By optimising routes, consolidating cargo,
and reducing the number of entries into the 500M zone, minimises fuel consumption, which in turn reduces
CO2 emissions.
Secondly, the use of real-time tracking and monitoring allows for more precise operations, reducing
unnecessary manoeuvring and minimising waste. The AI-based system's ability to predict and plan the most
efficient routes further ensures minimal fuel consumption and, therefore, minimal emissions. By optimising
transportation schedules and routes, consolidating cargo, and maximising back deck utilisation, the AI-
based platform can significantly reduce fuel consumption. The data provided in the study reveals that fuel
consumption decreased from 77 MT to 49 MT, representing a remarkable drop. The case study also reports
a reduction of CO2 emissions from 250 MT to 159 MT, amounting to a total reduction of 91 MT or 36%.
This reduction not only signifies cost savings but also aligns with global initiatives to reduce greenhouse gas
emissions, highlighting the sustainability advantage. For every 1,000 square meters shipped, the reduction in
CO2 emissions translates into savings of $5,460. This is significant, considering the scale at which offshore
operations take place, and these savings add up to substantial amounts over time.
Moreover, by reducing the delivery time by 23%, less energy is expended per operation, which also
contributes to the overall sustainability of the process. This efficiency has led to a significant reduction in
distance travelled, from 1,070 nautical miles to 527 nautical miles, a reduction of 51%, saving 68 hours
of sailing time.
Lastly, by providing a platform for better collaboration among stakeholders, The solution allows for
shared resources and synchronised operations, reducing the redundancy and waste often seen in isolated
operations.
Additionally, the sustainability benefits of the systems-integrated logistics approach have positive ripple
effects on other areas of the offshore industry. Companies can better meet regulatory requirements and
improve their environmental reputation by reducing environmental impact. This, in turn, can lead to
increased trust and confidence from stakeholders, potentially opening up new business opportunities.
In conclusion, the adoption of the systems-integrated logistics approach leads to a more sustainable
offshore operations environment. Not only does it reduce the sector's carbon footprint significantly, but it
also ensures that resources are utilised efficiently, contributing to long-term sustainability in the sector.

Procurement Behavior Change
Procurement is a crucial aspect of any logistics system, with direct impacts on cost, efficiency, and overall
performance. The system influences procurement behaviour in several ways, mainly by promoting data-led
decision-making and optimising material flows and operational programs.
Traditionally, procurement decisions in offshore logistics have been guided by experience, intuition,
and, often, a degree of guesswork. However, the solutions AI and machine learning capabilities allow
procurement teams to leverage data to make more accurate, informed decisions. For example, procurement
teams can use data from the AI-based platform to identify the most efficient routes, the most cost-effective
transportation modes, and the optimal times for shipments.
The system's predictive capabilities can also forecast future demand for various items, enabling
procurement teams to adjust their ordering practices accordingly. This can prevent over-ordering and under-
ordering, both of which can lead to increased costs and inefficiencies.
Furthermore, the systems focus on cargo planning and considers the relationship between material flows
and the operational programs they support. This means procurement teams are better equipped to understand
how their decisions will impact the entire supply chain. They can plan purchases in such a way that materials
arrive just when they are needed, reducing the need for warehousing and reducing the risk of delays in the
operational schedule.
By aligning procurement activities with the overall logistics strategy, the system enables companies to
maximise the value of their procurement spend. This not only results in cost savings but also leads to
improvements in operational efficiency and overall supply chain performance.
Discussion on the Systems Approach to Cargo Planning
Cargo planning is a critical aspect of supply chain management, particularly in the offshore sector, where the
movement of goods and equipment often involves multiple modes of transportation and complex schedules.
The system's innovative approach to cargo planning is a significant factor in its success and a major driver
of its efficiency and cost savings.
One key feature of the solutions approach to cargo planning is its utilisation of an AI-based platform.
This platform uses cargo and telemetric data to create both short and long-term schedules for the movement
of goods. This sophisticated planning process considers various factors such as cargo size, weight, type,
destination, and the most efficient transportation mode.
Through its AI and machine learning capabilities, The systems platform can optimise cargo consolidation,
enabling multiple shipments to be combined into a single transportation mode. This approach maximises
vessel utilisation and reduces the number of entries into the 500M zone, contributing to both operational
efficiency and safety improvements.
The systems cargo planning approach also considers the relationship between material flows and the
operational programs they support. This perspective ensures that the timing of cargo deliveries aligns
with operational schedules, minimising downtime and maximising productivity. It also helps to reduce
warehousing needs, as materials can be delivered just when they're needed.
Furthermore, the solutions AI-based platform offers real-time tracking and monitoring of the cargo.
This allows for instant visibility into the status and location of the goods, enabling quick reactions to
any disruptions and ensuring timely delivery. It also provides transparency to all stakeholders, leading to
improved decision-making and collaboration.
Conclusion
As evidenced by this study, the system exhibits immense potential in transforming integrated logistics within
the offshore sector. Its strategic use of artificial intelligence (AI) and digital solutions has redefined the
parameters of efficiency, safety, and cost-effectiveness in offshore logistics operations.

AI and digital solutions serve as the backbone of the platform, effectively addressing the industry's
perennial challenges, such as operational inefficiencies, safety risks, and high costs. By leveraging these
technologies, the systems infuses data-driven insights and automation into logistics processes, setting the
stage for continuous improvement and optimisation.
The study further reveals that the platform's influence extends beyond enhancing efficiency. It has
revolutionised safety and transparency within offshore logistics, primarily through its real-time tracking and
monitoring features. These capabilities minimise risks and provide actionable data, enabling stakeholders
to make informed decisions that advance their operational objectives.
Moreover, this strikes a balance between economic viability and environmental stewardship. Its
integration of AI and machine learning has unlocked significant cost savings. Concurrently, the system's
optimisation of resource planning and utilisation has dramatically reduced CO2 emissions, underscoring its
commitment to sustainability.
The implications of the success of the system are profound for the oil and gas industry. With sustainability
and cost-effectiveness as key imperatives in today's fast-paced world, the platform offers a new way for
the industry to rethink its supply chain operations. The system sets a benchmark for future offshore sector
operations by aligning with these imperatives.
In conclusion, the systems approach manifests the transformative potential of integrated logistics in the
offshore sector. Through the effective use of AI and digital solutions, it tackles the industry's longstanding
challenges head-on, catalysing improvements in efficiency, safety, and transparency. The system's delivery
of substantial cost savings, coupled with its significant environmental benefits, underscores its value
proposition. As such, the solution provides a compelling blueprint for re-imagining supply chain operations
in the oil and gas industry, ensuring that they are both sustainable and cost-effective in an ever-evolving
world.
